real_insight: When you find yourself romantic about quitting in the same week that a hard project lands on your plate, separate the two questions explicitly: decide on the project on its own terms; decide on quitting on its own terms.
objection: I have watched this happen a dozen times — the engineer who gets the deprecation assignment and within two weeks is interviewing at a smaller company, telling friends a poetic story about why. The verse names the asymmetry with surgical precision: the fantasised escape is undertaxed, the fantasised post-action life is overtaxed. That is the precise cognitive distortion I have not had a clean name for. The implication ('separate the two questions explicitly') is genuinely actionable; it would change behavior. The falsifiability protects against using this verse to talk people out of legitimate career moves. PASS. The quotable line 'the fantasised escape is always undertaxed; the fantasised damage is always overdramatised' is one I will remember.
